name: Factory Method / Abstract Factory
def: Defers object creation to a method/factory so callers depend on an interface, not a concrete class.
problem: Decouple code from the concrete types it instantiates, enabling families of related objects.
mechanism: A creator exposes a factory method returning an abstract product; subclasses or an abstract factory choose the concrete product family.
limits: Often replaced by DI or a closure
whenNot: Simple, stable construction (just call the constructor)
myths: "Factories are always good practice" — frequently a closure or DI container is simpler
